name: mock
context: Run a mock HTTP API from an OpenAPI contract for frontend, integration, demo, and CI workflows.
info: OpenAPI mocking serves fake API responses directly from a contract, usually before or beside a real backend. The primary execution surface is Stoplight Prism CLI, which can run `prism mock` against OpenAPI v2, v3.0, and v3.1 documents. Agents use this skill to unblock frontend development, simulate third-party APIs, test clients without real credentials, reproduce examples from docs, and validate whether a contract contains enough examples and schemas to support realistic behavior. Mocking should use a validated and bundled spec so route discovery, server URLs, examples, and refs behave predictably.
triggers: run a mock server from openapi
create a fake api for frontend
body:
# OpenAPI Mocking

Mocking turns a contract into an HTTP server. This is useful when the frontend is ahead of the backend, an upstream API is expensive or rate-limited, or a test needs deterministic responses without real credentials.

## Start a Mock Server

```bash
# Validate and bundle first
nodes/openapi/scripts/validate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml dist/openapi.yaml

# Start Prism on port 4010
nodes/openapi/scripts/mock-openapi.sh dist/openapi.yaml 4010

# Call the mock
curl http://localhost:4010/health
```

The wrapper uses a local `prism` binary if present, otherwise `npx --yes @stoplight/prism-cli`.

## Host and Port

```bash
OPENAPI_MOCK_HOST=127.0.0.1 OPENAPI_MOCK_PORT=5000 nodes/openapi/scripts/mock-openapi.sh openapi.yaml
```

Bind to `0.0.0.0` inside containers or remote dev environments when another process needs to reach the mock.

## Examples Drive Quality

Prism can use examples and schemas to produce responses. If the mock returns generic or unrealistic payloads, improve the contract by adding named examples under media types or by tightening schemas with required fields, formats, enums, and bounds.

```yaml
responses:
  '200':
    description: Customer
    content:
      application/json:
        schema:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/Customer'
        examples:
          active:
            value:
              id: cus_123
              email: alice@example.com
```

## Mocking in Tests

Run Prism in the background during integration tests, point the client base URL at the mock, and keep the spec file versioned with the test fixtures. Prefer contract examples over ad hoc JSON fixtures so docs, mocks, and tests share one source.

sections.title: Mocking workflow
sections.body: Validate and bundle the spec, start Prism with `nodes/openapi/scripts/mock-openapi.sh`, set the application or generated client base URL to the mock server, then run frontend or integration tests. When behavior is missing, update examples and schemas in [[openapi/author]] rather than patching the mock output separately.

sections.title: Examples as mock fixtures
sections.body: Good OpenAPI examples become mock fixtures, docs samples, and agent-readable behavior descriptions. Add named examples for common states: empty list, successful create, validation failure, unauthorized, not found, and rate-limited. Keep secrets and real customer data out of examples.

sections.title: Containers and remote workspaces
sections.body: Prism binds to localhost by default. In Docker, Codespaces, or remote dev containers, bind to `0.0.0.0` and expose the port. The wrapper defaults to `0.0.0.0` so agents can share the mock URL with nearby processes.

sections.title: Mock limits
sections.body: A mock server cannot replace a real implementation for persistence, authorization semantics, race conditions, background jobs, or database constraints. Use [[openapi/contract-test]] against a real service before release. Use mocks to unblock integration and clarify contracts, not to prove production behavior.
